reference: Hello. Before discussing the progression of scoliosis, I would like to clarify some facts about it. one) The Cobb angle is a measure of the curvature of the spine in degrees. This helps to determine what type of treatment is necessary. A Cobb angle of ten degrees is regarded as the minimum angulation to define scoliosis. two) A scoliosis curve of ten to fifteen degrees normally does not require any treatment except for regular check-ups. three) If the scoliosis curve is twenty to forty degrees, the orthopedic doctor will prescribe a back brace to help keep the spine from developing more of a curve. four) Curves greater than forty-five degrees require corrective surgery involving many pedicle screws and osteotomies to correct the curvature. I hope you have an X-ray done of your spine, as it will be easy to measure the curvature properly.
text: After that I worked at a gas station, then I worked at a knife factory, then I worked at a picture frame factory.
